hits.content:
###### The Wall Comedy Club

[![Live from the Wall Comedy Club - It's Saturday Night!!!](/media/user_3ad6d03b-a828-477b-84db-a8d839394b0e/1773609727.826206/event_image.jpg)](/events/2026-05-02-the-wall-comedy-live-from-the-wall-comedy-club-its-saturday-night/)

##### Live from the Wall Comedy Club - It's Saturday Night!!!

[The Wall Comedy](/venues/thewallcomedy/) • May 2 8:00 PM

[The Wall Comedy Productions](/producers/thewallcomedy/)

#### About This Event

A stand up comedy special featuring the best performers Berlin has to offer! Join us for an unforgettable evening!

Show is in English

Doors 8pm

Show 8:30pm

The Wall Comedy Club was founded on a simple idea, that literally everything can turn into a joke that can help make people's days a bit brighter. From our capacity (69 lol), to our mistake in asking the German government for permission to build a new Berlin Wall, (and they approved for some reason!), we'd like to invite you to a celebration of how far the stand up comedy scene has grown in the last few years in Berlin.

What can you expect from the show? We've invited some of the best international comedians who perform regularly in Berlin and throughout Europe for a two hour stand up comedy show. We have a lovely bar with drinks and cocktails and will take a 15 minute intermission roughly half way through the show. If you want to see some of the best Stand Up Comedy in Berlin, this event is for you!

About the Club: The Wall Comedy was founded in January 2020, truly the best (or worst if you have ever heard of something called "the news") time. We have stand up shows every day apart from Monday and all the shows are in English except for one show on Sundays which is in German. Our goal is to help grow the stand up community in English and create a truly open space for comedians to shine.

What about refunds? Do you do them? And Can I pay with a credit card? Yes to both! We ask that if you request a refund, you give at least one-day notice so we have time to process it. If you try to refund too close to the event, we might not see it and can't refund a ticket within a couple hours of the event. Oh, and we definitely take cards.

What are the ticket options/what if I want to buy at the door? We can sell you tickets at the door, but only if we aren't sold out. If you see the event is sold out online, we won't have any tickets available at the door. However, if we do have open space, we sell tickets for €20 at the door! And we have an early bird ticket which you can buy for the old price up to a week in advance for the show or a group ticket that you can buy with groups of 4 or larger.

Where are we located and when do we open? We are located right at Boxhagener Platz in Friedrichshain. We open at 8pm and the show will start at 8:30pm. If you are looking for something to do before the show, there is park nearby and a ton of restaurants and activities. We are even close to the East Side Gallery if you want to look at Berlin's original Wall before dropping by the Wall Comedy Club!

We look forward to seeing you!

€12 - €15

[Sign up as a performer](/accounts/signup/performer/ "Sign up to perform")

### The Wall Comedy

The Wall Comedy Club, Berlin was ironically based on a world with no more walls and is a tribute to the fall of the Berlin Wall in 1989. It is a place where anyone can come visit to share in our common sense of laughter and fun with some of the best comedians and artists from all over the world! …

* Email: [info@thewallcomedy.com](mailto:info@thewallcomedy.com)
* Web: [https://www.eventbrite.com/o/…](https://www.eventbrite.com/o/the-wall-comedy-33895977485 "https://www.eventbrite.com/o/the-wall-comedy-33895977485")

Address

Grünberger Str. 84

Berlin , Berlin 10245

hits.snippet:
From April 7, 2026 to March 2, 2027 all fans are invited to unforgettable concerts with live sound by the inimitable Valery Meladze! After the resounding success of the 2025 tour, he returns to the European stage once again.Valery Meladze concerts in Germany will be held in such cities as Berlin, Hamburg, Munich and many others. Fans are waiting for time-tested hits, premieres of new compositions and just a sea of emotions.Legendary romantic of the 90's: favorite hits in a new soundValery Meladze is an artist whose creativity has long gone beyond the boundaries of one era. More than 30 years have passed since the release of his debut solo song "Don't disturb my soul, violin...", but he continues to please his fans with more and more new hits.Within the framework of the tour 2026-2027 [...] Buy tickets

Ekaterina Varnava and Svetlana Ustinova in "Chanel vs. Rubinstein" in Berlin from 19 May 2026 353

# The comedy show "Vecherniy Berlin" (ru)24 November 2025

Image 1: The comedy show 

Add to favourites)

"Vecherniy Berlin" (ru) - the first late-night show in Russian in Berlin!

What it means. This show will ironically and curiously discuss the top news, city life and get to know the Berliners who make the capital special. All in all, taking inspiration from Conan, Colbert and Urgant, there will be a program about the real Berlin. 

To discuss the current news of Berlin, GENAU online media in the person of Dima Vachedin will join.

This time our guests will be:

"Vecherniy Berlin" (ru) - the first late-night show in Russian in Berlin!

What it means. This show will ironically and curiously discuss the top news, city life and get to know the Berliners who make the capital special. All in all, taking inspiration from Conan, Colbert and Urgant, there will be a program about the real Berlin. 

To discuss the current news of Berlin, GENAU online media in the person of Dima Vachedin will join.

This time our guests will be:

*   Ilya Krasilshchik - former editor of Afisha, creator of Yandex Lavka, social activist, admin of Der Immigrant channel
*   Singer Vera Musaelyan from the band AloeVera!

There will be shooting, please do not be late!

Hosts: Max Aslanov, Senya Zaika

On the sound: Anton Komandirov

Ekaterina Varnava and Svetlana Ustinova are together for the first time on stage - in a musical performance inspired by the events of the past.Two of the brightest stars of modern theater and cinema - Ekaterina Varnava and Svetlana Ustinova - meet in a drama based on real historical events. Their duo turns the story of Chanel, Rubinstein and Prince Dmitry Romanov into a powerful emotional duel, where each scene is a clash of characters, temperaments and destinies.Ekaterina Varnava creates a fragile and daring image of Ida Rubinstein - Diaghilev's muse, ballerina, patroness and the woman who inspired Maurice Ravel to create the immortal "Bolero".Svetlana Ustinova embodies the complex and multifaceted Gabrielle Chanel - the woman who changed the aesthetics of the 20th century and created the legendary Chanel No. 5 fragrance.The audience will be treated to:the birth of Chanel No. 5 fragrance,the creation of Ravel's Bolero,the conflict between two great women,the fate of Prince Dmitry Romanov, a man caught between two worlds.Together with Varnava and Ustinova on stage - Dmitry Kondrashov, giving the play additional depth and drama.The audience will find themselves in the very center of meetings, quarrels, reconciliations and revelations between three iconic figures whose influence is still shaping the cultural landscape of Europe.Playwright and director: Olga Henkina

After the resounding success of the 2025 tour, he returns to the European stage once again.Valery Meladze concerts in Germany will be held in such cities as Berlin, Hamburg, Munich and many others. Fans are waiting for time-tested hits, premieres of new compositions and just a sea of emotions.Legendary romantic of the 90's: favorite hits in a new soundValery Meladze is an artist whose creativity has long gone beyond the boundaries of one era. More than 30 years have passed since the release of his debut solo song "Don't disturb my soul, violin...", but he continues to please his fans with more and more new hits.Within the framework of the tour 2026-2027 Meladze will present a program adapted to modern stage technologies. High-quality visual effects, powerful sound and, of course, incredible energy of the singer himself make each concert of Valery Meladze not just a show, but a full-fledged artistic experience.Why do even young people come to see Meladze? How old hits take on new meaning in the TikTok eraModern youth actively discovers the artist's creativity through social networks. Many compositions received a "second life", becoming part of viral trends, as well as remixes in the style of rock and others. So now Meladze and each of his concerts attract not only longtime fans, but also a new generation of listeners.From "Sera", "White Moth Samba" and "High Society Girls" to such compositions as "My Brother", "I See the Sun", "Loved" and "Heaven" - in any era Valery's songs remain relevant. Audiences come to his performances for the insane energy, passion and sincerity that is in every note!Meladze's songs in Germany: when music does not require translation"At Valery's concerts in Germany an amazing thing happens: even those who don't know Russian, confidently sing along to familiar motifs. The secret is simple - the incredible melody and drive of Meladze's songs tug at the heartstrings and unite the audience faster than any dictionary.Magic of the orchestra: from pop music to neoclassicsThe tour pays special attention to symphonic sound. The use of orchestral arrangements adds a new dimension to the compositions, bringing them closer to neoclassical aesthetics and even to the works of composers such as Einaudi or Morricone. This allows you to look at familiar songs from a new angle.What awaits the audience: Meladze tours 2026-2027Every concert of Valery Meladze's new European tour will bring fans from all over Germany and beyond. Theatres[](javascript:void(0))

Directed by Avdotya SmirnovaStarring: Kseniya Rappoport, Henry DavidDramaturgy: Olga FedyaninaSet and Costume Design: Ksenia PeretrukhinaMovement: Polina DreydenVideo: Nik DreydenLight: Gleb FilshtinskyThe play "Beyond the Light" is a collaborative work by renowned writer Marina Stepnova and screenwriter and director Avdotya Smirnova. It is a story of an unexpected meeting between two unusual characters, each of whom, until a certain point, is unaware of their own uniqueness.What will happen when two excentric personalities collide in a place, where it seems, they don't belong to? Their coincidental encounter and the connection between them unfold unpredictably. She is a vibrant soul, an earthly woman from an entirely different planet. He is an algorithm which is slowly acquiring a human face. The roles are reversed, and where there once was chaos hope begins to grow. In the new world, pieced together from the shards of the old, everything feels at once familiar and grotesque — funny and sad.The space, which is imagined by set designer Ksenia Peretrukhina, reinforces this atmosphere: a fantastical world is born from everyday objects — a world made of fragments of ordinary life, forming a new mosaic, paradoxical yet logical at the same time. Here, absurdity and reality walk hand in hand.In the lead role Kseniya Rappoport — flesh and blood, the very heartbeat of the magical act in which the woman triumphs over death.Her partner is Henry David, a second-generation star of the Gesher Theatre and a well-known actor of Israeli cinema, who complements and balances this magnificent duo.Together, they bring unique theatrical alchemy to the stage.Their interaction is not only the plotline but a profound metaphysical journey, in which they discover their path to the truth through passion, pain, and redemption.

Theatres[](javascript:void(0))

"Dog Heart" this fall on the theatrical stages of German cities!The iconic play "Dog's Heart" directed by the famous Valery Zolotukhin - on the 100th anniversary of the writing of the satirical novel of the same name by Mikhail Bulgakov. Valery Zolotukhin's production is rightfully recognized as one of the best. Grandiose premiere from the creators of the sensational play "The Master and Margarita" - Bulgakov Theatre, successfully held in German cities in 2019, 2021 and 2024!"Dog's Heart"Mikhail Bulgakov writes the story in the 20s of the last century, when the time of the heroes has passed, and there remained everyday life, which is the test for a person. This is the height of the construction of socialism, the eternal apartment issue, the devastation in the houses and, most importantly - in the heads.The "Dog's Heart", as well as many other works of genius Bulgakov - a unique fate. The work was written back in 1925, but for more than 60 years was under a ban, and was distributed only in samizdat. The release of the film with E. Evstigneev in the title role, received universal acclaim and attracted not only new readers, but also aroused the interest of many prominent theater directors who offered their interpretation of the legendary Bulgakov text. Valery Zolotukhin's production is rightfully recognized as one of the best.So, as a result of one of the most complicated experiments, Professor Philip Preobrazhensky makes a stunning discovery: after a pituitary gland transplant, his test dog Sharik turns into a human! The sensational news brings the world-famous professor recognition. However, the joy of the professor-experimenter is short-lived: the question that Sharik - later citizen Poligraf Poligrafych Sharikov - can become a "high psychic personality" is quickly called into question. Bulgakov's vivid satire is conveyed in the play with surprising grace and conviction.The castIn the character of Professor Preobrazhensky is the unsurpassed Igor Khalizov, theater and film actor, director, familiar to the audience from the films "The Return of Mukhtar", "On Different Shores", "Dissident", as well as from his brilliant role of Voland in the play "The Master and Margarita".The role of Sharikov is played by the magnificent Leonid Grinberg, a theater and film actor and a star of Broadway musicals.The role of Bormental is played by Boris Bogdanov, a graduate of VGIK (workshop of V. B. Ahadov), an actor of the Narva theater "Ilmarine", familiar to the audience from the films "What Men Talk About", "Anka from Moldovanka", "Balabol".The image of Shvonder is embodied by Valentin Melnyk, a graduate of the Kiev National University of Culture and Arts, who played in the popular projects "Return of Mukhtar", "Merry Quarter", "Supercops", "Defectives".Also in the play are theater and film actors Elizaveta Lebed, Anastasia Grishchuk and Oleksiy Avramenko.

No, it's not a musical. It is a dramatic story, born in a quiet intertwining of destinies under the light of old Berlin lanterns.And yes, it is a live, you know, a little bit such a chamber concert of Anna Herman's songs. After all, our protagonist is a singer. And the hero seems to be her guardian angel.The plot... A little mystical, but at the same time absolutely worldly. And that's the beauty of its tenderness, sometimes - pain to tears, and, where without it - laughter through those very tears.This performance is like a letter to yourself, written by your soul to the best music of all times. It is about love that comes suddenly. It's about two people whose hearts found each other against all odds. What's "everything"? They'll tell you. They'll even sing. In a way that will make you want to sing with them. And not only to sing. But also to dream, to think, to rejoice, and most importantly - to feel alive!We dedicate this performance:To lovers, to be filled with this feeling again and again.To those who are waiting for a meeting to tell you: it is possible, even when it is impossible.And to those who love Anna Herman as much as we do.If you like movies, plays and books that leave a pleasant flurry on your soul with a slight note of nostalgia and a reviving faith in what makes you happy - definitely, do you hear? This diversity of cultural backgrounds combines to form a strong artistic unit that unites technical precision with expressive stage presence.At the heart of the production is the timeless music of Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky, whose emotional depth and dramatic power are brought out particularly vividly in this interpretation. The tragic love story of Prince Siegfried and Odette is told with great sensitivity, clear classical lines and fine psychological nuance. The choreography remains committed to tradition and at the same time unfolds a fresh, lively dynamic.A key feature of this production is its visual design. All the costumes have been newly designed and manufactured with the utmost craftsmanship. Fine materials, detailed embellishments and harmoniously coordinated color schemes underline the poetic atmosphere of the work. The stage sets are painted entirely by hand and create impressive scenic spaces - from the mysterious lake to the magnificent court - that immerse the audience directly in the world of classical ballet.With an ensemble of around 30 performers, the Solenne Ballet Classique offers a production suitable for touring at an international level. The technical concept is designed in such a way that both large theaters and established guest performance venues can be optimally served.Solenne Ballet Classique stands for artistic discipline, international cooperation and a passion for making the classical repertoire accessible to a broad European audience in a high-quality form. The upcoming tour promises impressive evenings full of grace, drama and visual splendor - a ballet classic in a new light.

For the first time in Germany! Premiere of the show-performance "Men do not dance striptease"!Supercomedy in two acts from the creators of the plays "Dog's Heart", "Master and Margarita", "Viy", which have toured with great success in theaters in Europe, America, Switzerland and other countries of the world.A bright, funny and very touching story about men who dared to do the impossible. A special forces officer, a former bandit, a musician and a bankrupt businessman. Four grown men, each with their own fate, mistakes and losses, find themselves at a point where there is nothing more to lose: no job, no money, no self-confidence. Their only chance to change their lives is risky and, at first glance, absurd: to try to get into an elite male strip show. They are embarrassed, scared, and ridiculous all at the same time. But this very path becomes an opportunity for them to believe in themselves anew, to overcome complexes and learn to be a team.While the four "men" learn to dance and step by step go beyond the usual, the audience laughs heartily. But behind the light comedy form there is an honest and deep conversation about manhood, the right to weakness, a second chance and the fact that you can start over at any age.The finale of the play is a bright, extravaganza show filled with emotions, self-irony and unexpected tenderness.It's a story about love - first of all for a woman,but even more - for LIFE.Directed by Dmitry RachkovskyGenre: extravaganza comedyForm: a performance in two acts with intermission

Theatres[](javascript:void(0))

The world premiere of the play "INOSTRANKA" based on the novel of the same name by Sergei Dovlatov.Directed by Maxim DidenkoStarring Masha MashkovaBased on real events.What makes a person leave home? Politics, history, circumstances - or just a "bad mood"? The play "Inostranka" based on the story by Sergei Dovlatov seeks answers to these questions and transfers them to today's reality. But this is not just the story of the heroine Marusya Tatarovich - it is also the personal story of actress Masha Mashkova, her attempts to make her way in Hollywood, life in Los Angeles, memories of family and growing up."Inostranka" is a play-talk without a fourth wall: ironic, honest, funny and therapeutic.It speaks about emigration as an eternal human experience - an attempt to find one's place in this world and keep a sense of humor when everything around you becomes foreign and new.If in the 80s emigration was a domestic drama, today it is an existential leap into the unknown. But the essence remains the same: each of us one day becomes a "foreigner" - in a new country, profession or even our own life. "Let's summarize the facts. A certain Maria leaves her homeland. Then Maria, you see, asks to go back. It seems as if homeland is a variable for some people. If I want to leave, if I change my mind, I'll come back. In the meantime, a heinous betrayal has been committed, I apologize. Which means we must make amends. And then it will be decided whether to let you back in. Or not to let you back." Sergei Dovlatov. Story Inostranka. 1985."I have long been looking for material, thanks to which the audience in the theater will be able to snehohochat all the accumulated. Up to tears prohohochat. And to me came the heroine Marusya (drum roll) from Novosibirsk! (who doesn't know, I lived until I was 8 years old in the airline town of Tolmachevo), who emigrated to America, but the life of an emigrant... you know what I mean. And Marusya now very suddenly wanted to go back. I turned page after page of the story with shaking hands to find out sooner if Marusya would come back... back." Masha MashkovaThe author's method of director Maxim Didenko combines live theater, cinema and multimedia, creating a modern language of stage art and opening upnew forms of theatrical expression. There are two parallel processes in the performance: the theatrical, staging, and cinematic. The movie itself becomes part of the performance, which gives the project a special depth.Maxim Sukhanov, Varya Shmykova, Nikita Kukushkin and Nikola Ashurkov starred in "INOSTRANKA" .Authors of the play:Playwright - Mikhail DurnenkovDirector - Maxim DidenkoComposer - Anna DrubichCostumes - Tatiana DolmatovskayaProduction designer - Ksenia PeretrukhinaLighting and sound design - Yuri GalkinMultimedia - Oleg MikhailovProducers - Alexandrina Markvo, Katerina Kashintseva, Masha MashkovaStage Manager - Ilya Kuznetsov

hits.snippet:
Alexander Nezlobin's European Tour 2026 is a new chapter in the work of one of the most recognizable comedians of the Russian-speaking scene. The concert program "I'm Fine" is an honest, ironic and very personal stand-up about life after moving, adaptation in a new country and trying to keep himself in the changing reality.

In the new concert, Alexander Nezlobin talks about the family that is close by, about emigration without heroization and pathos, about everyday trifles that suddenly turn into complex quests, and about his daughter, who navigates in a new country faster than adults. These are stories about fears, doubts and situations that at first are not funny, but after a while - very funny.

On February 18, the project will be filmed in Munich. [...] On February 18, the project will be filmed in Munich.

"I'm Fine" - a standup about the state of "like everything is normal", familiar to everyone who started life from scratch. Nezlobin's humor remains recognizable: observant, intelligent, accurate and as human as possible. Without moralizing and loud conclusions - only living stories, in which the audience can easily recognize themselves.

Alexander Nezlobin is a stand-up comic, scriptwriter and director, one of the first residents of Comedy Club and a key figure in the development of modern Russian-language humor. His work is equally close to millennials and zoomers - those who formulate an impression short and succinct: "plus vibe". [...] Gießen,

# Alexander Nezlobin. European Tour 2026

![event-image](/build/frontend/images/bg_img.png)

**Alexander Nezlobin goes on European Tour 2026 with the concert "I'm Fine"**

Alexander Nezlobin's European Tour 2026 is a new chapter in the work of one of the most recognizable comedians of the Russian-speaking scene. The concert program "I'm Fine" is an honest, ironic and very personal stand-up about life after moving, adapting to a new country and trying to keep himself in the changing reality.

![event-image](https://imgcdnv2.kartina.stream/bilet/prod/activity/1039/big_20260421125416.jpg?action=resize&width=250)

### Standup: women's microphone in Berlin

**Alexander Nezlobin goes on European Tour 2026 with the concert "I'm Fine"**

Alexander Nezlobin's European Tour 2026 is a new chapter in the work of one of the most recognizable comedians of the Russian-speaking scene. The concert program "I'm Fine" is an honest, ironic and very personal stand-up about life after moving, adaptation in a new country and trying to keep himself in the changing reality.

In the new concert, Alexander Nezlobin talks about the family that is close by, about emigration without heroization and pathos, about everyday trifles that suddenly turn into complex quests, and about his daughter, who navigates in a new country faster than adults. These are stories about fears, doubts and situations that at first are not funny, but after a while - very funny.

On February 18, the **project** will be **filmed** in Munich.

"I'm Fine" - a standup about the state of "like everything is normal", familiar to everyone who started life from scratch. Nezlobin's humor remains recognizable: observant, intelligent, accurate and as human as possible. Without moralizing and loud conclusions - only living stories, in which the audience can easily recognize themselves.

Alexander Nezlobin is a stand-up comic, scriptwriter and director, one of the first residents of Comedy Club and a key figure in the development of modern Russian-language humor. His work is equally close to millennials and zoomers - those who formulate an impression short and succinct: "plus vibe".

# Garik Oganisyan in Germany. Stand-up tour "Here and There"from 19 January 2026 to 7 February 2026

![Image 1: Garik Oganisyan in Germany. Stand-up tour ](https://www.kontramarka.de/upload/img/86056_432h6101.jpg)

[Add to favourites](javascript:void(0))

Garik Oganisyan in Germany! One of the most witty comedians of the post-Soviet space, emigrant and hedonist is going on tour around the country with a new solo concert.

Garik Oganisyan - standup comedian, emigrant and hedonist - will be on stage as a standup star, master of puns and just a wonderful person. The name of this talented comedian is well known to all connoisseurs of quality humor. Garik Oganisyan is the star of the cult Stand Up Club #1, a participant of many YouTube projects, the founder of a stand-up club in Yerevan and just a kind and funny person. His performances, in which sparkling jokes, incredibly funny puns and life stories are merged into a fascinating story filled with self-irony and unexpected twists, leave no one indifferent.

Standup fans are eagerly awaiting the release of his new numbers and programs. And the comedian doesn't let them get bored for long! Not so long ago Garik Oganisyan released two solo shows: "Life is the Best Playwright" on YouTube and "Life is the Worst Playwright".

Seminars[](javascript:void(0))

Becoming successful and happy is everyone's wish. However, not everyone and not always manages to achieve success.But Irina Hakamada is sure that there is nothing difficult about it. Using her personal life experience and knowledge, combining Eastern philosophy, Western business approaches and post-Soviet culture, she created her own recipe that helped her become not only successful, but also happy. She is not only ready to share it, but also asserts that there is nothing complicated about it, no special settings or material goods are required.During her exclusive speeches in Germany and France, Irina Hakamada will talk about how to develop motivation, raise the standard of living, build relationships with loved ones, minimize conflicts, prevent burnout, find a vocation, achieve success and personal growth, find harmony and happiness. Irina is able to explain any complex things fantastically simple. She does not pretend to be a guru, does not seek to manipulate the public's consciousness, to give them any attitudes and advice.Irina offers to listen to her and try to do the things that she did in her time. Her stories are worth listening to. Participation in the master class will definitely help you find answers to many questions and change your idea of what it means to be successful. Come for ideas and interesting communication!Don't miss it! All those who bought tickets to the event will be able to buy Irina Hakamada's autographed books.

Audiences appreciate Ilya's sincerity and ability to talk about serious things with ease and a kind smile.Ilya's concert is not just stand-up, but a conversation filled with love of humor, respect for the audience and vivid observations about our reality. At his concerts laughter is combined with hope and faith in the best, and the audience leaves with the feeling that they have become part of something bigger.Ilya Axelrod's concert in Berlin is a cool event that will give everyone an unforgettable evening full of humor, warmth and energy.

Hi everyone! I’m here with an English speaking show recommendation ![Image 1: 💫](https://static.xx.fbcdn.net/images/emoji.php/v9/t8c/2/16/1f4ab.png)

On Saturday the 17th you can see a standup show from a comedian based in berlin Sasha DOLGOPOLOV. 

They are a famous russian-speaking comedian who live in Europe since 2022 and tours worldwide with hilarious shows ![Image 2: 🫶🏻](https://static.xx.fbcdn.net/images/emoji.php/v9/t63/2/16/1faf6_1f3fb.png)

•how do you take care of yourself when the world around you is falling apart? 

•how do you balance survival and rest? how do you keep from exploding under pressure? 

•how do you stay calm when your life is unstable?

sasha is searching for answers and inviting us to join this intriguing and hilarious exploration, while also talking about migration, gender, sexuality, politics, language and growing up.

Where? ![Image 3: 📍](https://static.xx.fbcdn.net/images/emoji.php/v9/tcc/2/16/1f4cd.png) Bar For Sjov, Skolegade 26

When? ![Image 4: ⏱️](https://static.xx.fbcdn.net/images/emoji.php/v9/t36/2/16/23f1.png) 17.01, 19:00

Get your tickets here:

[https://fienta.com/sasha-dolgopolov-eng-156691](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Ffienta.com%2Fsasha-dolgopolov-eng-156691%3Futm_id%3D97758_v0_s00_e0_tv4&h=AT45rwNfXjWD-7vmAwxid1ii1y5BQVitHLeYVKMWp3eXf84qoxYyCFvgc3_fb2-Dgzb4Ts5Qg0ROAhpN6POgK5pvNrqwp-FKrKD682PxaQRLNG8H6jyMYUejcFf2inXw-H4lPIFxdPpiIPmP92PEjM1h1x6Qr3UzYULaBHO2PDtwazzXWDrFMQ&__tn__=-UK-R&c[0]=AT6SQzU6GkS7TIUCSF13i7NwWK9Hm9VPdyKUs_EsI5f86OOTVaQGRBuQsZLyYrIJkv5VHZo4L_bVu7nrKpBlqnPnIqO7foGTShuMuI4CMo6nRBlyJMWRw0gmP5g6fDTsdxifl1fVTmeIkqvofO6AmqAckvEtkLPcWWswtMe4vpkrJVsNgLK4onO0OESeSt4sJ90HhowqLVosr-OV3kYVTQHR6idv)

# Pasha Krivets in Germany. Stand-up tour "Good News"

event-image

Pasha Krivets is going on tour in Germany with the solo program "Good News". The concerts will take place in several cities and will be of interest to the Russian-speaking audience.

Pasha is a comedian, author and host of the show "Low Minds", as well as the projects "Lokal live", "Gutarki" and "Razgony". He works as a scriptwriter of comedy series and entertainment formats. The Good News program features new material on life, emigration and everyday situations with a signature pitch and improvisation.

### Idrak Mirzalizadeh in Europe. Stand Up "90% Halal"

![event-image](https://imgcdnv2.kartina.stream/bilet/prod/activity/1039/big_20260421125416.jpg?action=resize&width=250)

### Standup: women's microphone in Berlin

Pasha Krivets is going on tour in Germany with the solo program "Good News". The concerts will take place in several cities and will be of interest to the Russian-speaking audience.

Pasha is a comedian, author and host of the show "Low Minds", as well as the projects "Lokal live", "Gutarki" and "Razgony". He works as a scriptwriter of comedy series and entertainment formats. The program "Good News" features new material about life, emigration and everyday situations with his signature delivery and improvisation.

Come to the concerts - Pasha has "Good News" for you.

![Image 6](https://www.admiralspalast.theater/files/simpler/images-general/trans.gif)

HBDS - Russian Comedy

*   ![Image 7](https://www.admiralspalast.theater/veranstaltung/assets/images/c/HBDS_Foto_web-4e694ee9.jpg)

October 28, 2019 on the stage of “Admiralspalast" will be a breathtaking comedy show HBDS. The stars of the main comedy show in Russia Comedy Club will present their program. Masters of humor will communicate with the audience and improvise. Each performance is unique, so this you will not see and hear anywhere else. This is exclusive only in your city!

 HBDS shows are Garik Kharlamov, Timur Batrudinov, Demis Karibidis and Andrey Skorokhod. It is these ingredients that will make it possible to create an immediate humor bomb that can burst with laughter in a matter of seconds.

 More than an hour of real interactivity with the audience, improvisations, the best performance without the editors. Yes! And now only you have the opportunity to see what was never shown on TNT!

 “At our live concerts it’s always hot! Because we can show what is not included in the television version for reasons of censorship. So everything will not be so glamorously sleek with us, ”warns one of the participants of the show, Garik Kharlamov. You understand that the upcoming HBDS show is a challenge! If you are not afraid of daring jokes, appreciate bold and frank humor, then this is for you!

 In their speech, comedians will show the best and time-tested performance, but they will also be modernized. An overhaul is guaranteed: shocking parodies, jokes and funny, miniatures and songs for famous motives. This is a laugh to tears, so stock up on napkins in advance!

 Within the framework of one concert, you will find numerous meetings with representatives of different professions and areas: traffic police officers, officials and oligarchs, glamorous guys and girls, housewives and wives of oligarchs. Of course, you are waiting for meetings with other "celebrities", but you will learn about this at a concert, because unexpected meetings with your loved ones are always a joy.

 October 28, “Admiralspalast, Berlin, Germany, beginning at 20:00 do not miss your chance, because the top Comedy Club residents are coming to you. Allow yourself to forget about censorship for one evening, truly relax and recharge your batteries positive for a long time to come. If you appreciate the Comedy Club stars' humor, then buying tickets is a must, as there will be no second chance to see this performance.

Veranstalter: GNB Agency

_Derzeit keine Termine für diese Veranstaltung aufgenommen. Termine anderer Veranstaltungen buchbar..._

Sasha Dolgopolov in Europe: new English-language stand-up comedy show

Alternative queer comic and one of the most original figures of the contemporary Russian-language scene, Sasha Dolgopolov, presents a new English-language stand-up comedy show in Europe. **Sasha Dolgopolov in Europe: new English-language stand-up comedy show**

Alternative queer comic and one of the most original figures of the contemporary Russian-language scene, Sasha Dolgopolov, presents a new English-language stand-up comedy show in Europe. This is the comedian's second full-fledged tour in English - and a completely new program created after moving from Russia and starting his life in exile.

After last year's program on the subject of stress, Dolgopolov's new show explores the opposite state - relaxing, slowing down, accepting and trying to find calm in a world that is rapidly changing. The viewer is treated to an honest and paradoxical conversation about how to preserve yourself when the reality around you collapses; how to balance survival and relaxation; how not to explode under the pressure of uncertainty; and how to remain yourself even when you are not accepted.

The comedian tackles topics of migration, gender, sexuality, politics, growing up, language, and even touchingly absurd fruit flies - while maintaining a signature style that combines intelligence, sass, vulnerability, and a precise delivery rhythm. The show is performed entirely in English and is created for a wide international audience.

Sasha is an alternative political and queer comic from Russia who left the country after the war in Ukraine began. In ten years in standup, she has become one of the genre's most notable auteur figures: provocative, expressive, original and meaningful to a generation of viewers.

Dolgopolov's content on YouTube, Instagram and TikTok has racked up more than 10 million views. Seven solo hours, including one English-language one, have cumulatively garnered over 14 million views. In recent years, the comedian has performed in more than 24 countries, through festivals, competitions, open mic stages and hundreds of solo concerts.

The new tour is a step into the next chapter, where the artist reaches out to international audiences while maintaining her honesty, boldness and recognizable signature style.

hits.title: Alex Edelman, Award Winning Stand-up Comedian, Writer, and Jew
hits.snippet:
Edelman has been on the road with comedians like Ricky Gervais, Jack Whitehall, Patton Oswalt, Gary Gulman and a handful of musicians like Beck and San Fermin. In August of 2019, he was handpicked by Belle and Sebastian’s Stuart Murdoch to appear on the band’s Boaty Weekender, where he performed alongside Yo La Tengo, Buzzcocks, Alvvays, and many other bands as the outing’s lone comedian. He got a sunburn.   
  
In 2015, he travelled to Berlin and Moscow with Eddie Izzard to perform two shows to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the end of World War II.  
  
Beyond comedy: [...] —The Guardian

[Skip to Content](#page) 

[![Alex Edelman, Award Winning Stand-up Comedian, Writer, and Jew](//images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ec9852b9d9c412a9d842053/1594011915625-8C7DO85LEF43R6ERPLFO/IMG-5944.jpeg?format=1500w)](/)



![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ec9852b9d9c412a9d842053/61cc75e0-71c0-4788-8bd7-a66c8b1f1960/Alex+Edelman_WebsiteImage.png)

## Back on the road. With new material

OK. I’m slowly but happily trotting out a new show with new material. It’s called WHAT ARE YOU GOING TO DO for the time being. That might change, who knows. I’m excited and nervous. More cities will be added but for now it’s just these in the United States.

Live dates for it are below in the live section and keep checking, we’re always adding more dates.

For JUST FOR US stuff, btw.

A total accounting of its live journey is in the solo show section below and you can see the trailer [here.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qPsk1697UEo)

The Just For Us script is available for purchase and licensing through Dramatist Play Service. [Order here](https://www.dramatists.com/cgi-bin/db/single.asp?key=6639).

![MJP_7501.jpg](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ec9852b9d9c412a9d842053/1590283530691-3JU2MIWFU29JQIRGCORO/MJP_7501.jpg)

#### “I fondly imagined this was what it might have been like to watch Jerry Seinfeld when he was in his early 20s. There is no greater praise than that.”

— *The Mirror*

#### “Every story is a precision-engineered Rolls-Royce of a routine, perfectly timed and constructed, each morsel of information released just at the right moment.”

— *Chortle*

## Who

## Bostonian comedian. Jew. Sweetheart.

Alex Edelman’s first show, MILLENNIAL – about very traumatic stock photos and young people – won the Edinburgh Comedy Award for Best Newcomer, the first show by an American to do so since 1997. The follow-up, EVERYTHING HANDED TO YOU – about identity and the availability of outlets in airports – was even more acclaimed: selling out its entire Edinburgh run and garnering the second best reviews of any comedy show at the Festival.

His third show, JUST FOR US – which centers on a meeting of neo-Nazis that Edelman attended in New York – has cemented his reputation as a writer-performer of impressive ambition and technical skill. In its premiere run at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival, it was nominated for a Barry Award for Best Show. In Edinburgh, it earned a Herald Angel Award, a nomination for Best Show, and superlative praise that made it the best reviewed comedy show at the Festival in a decade.

Besides Edinburgh and Melbourne, JUST FOR US has had several extended runs in London’s West End at the Soho Theatre, the prestigious comedy haunt where many of Edelman’s solo efforts have premiered. The show has also been presented at Just For Laughs in Montreal, at the Bell House for New York Comedy Festival, Off-Broadway at the Lucille Lortel Theater, the Cherry Lane Theater, and the Soho Playhouse. JUST FOR US premiered on Broadway at the Hudson Theater summer 2023 and was later released as a special on Max. It won a special Tony. And an Emmy.

His album [UNTIL NOW](https://800pgr.lnk.to/EdelmanWE) - released in July of 2020 - was warmly received by critics and audiences alike, and was named one of the year’s best by NPR’s Bullseye.

**TV writing:**  
He’s recently completed a writing stint on the forthcoming Jenji Kohan-produced TEENAGE BOUNTY HUNTERS for Netflix. Prior to that, he served as a staff writer on THE GREAT INDOORS for CBS.  
  
Besides his narrative stuff, Edelman has written for countless awards shows, including the BRITs and the BAFTAs.

He is a troublesome Jew and the head writer and executive producer of SATURDAY NIGHT SEDER, a star studded 70-minute special, posted on YouTube, that has so far raised $3.5 million for the CDC Foundation (COVID-19) Emergency Response Fund. More than 1 million people watched the initial stream, which was hosted by Jason Alexander with participants that included Nick Kroll, Josh Groban, Rachel Brosnahan, Idina Menzel, Debra Messing and Bette Midler. You can watch SNS [here](https://www.saturdaynightseder.com).

**Outside his solo shows:**

Edelman has made numerous TV appearances in the US, Australia, and the UK.  
  
He has been featured at JFL as a New Face, appeared on CONAN multiple times, and is a staple on those “Comedians You Should Know” type lists. He is the creator of PEER GROUP – a show about the Millennial generation – on BBC Radio 4 and his hour-long special, LIVE AT THE BBC, is available on Netflix in some countries. He tours both nationally and internationally and is one of the founders of Jerusalem’s Off the Wall Comedy Club.

Edelman has been on the road with comedians like Ricky Gervais, Jack Whitehall, Patton Oswalt, Gary Gulman and a handful of musicians like Beck and San Fermin. In August of 2019, he was handpicked by Belle and Sebastian’s Stuart Murdoch to appear on the band’s Boaty Weekender, where he performed alongside Yo La Tengo, Buzzcocks, Alvvays, and many other bands as the outing’s lone comedian. He got a sunburn.   
  
In 2015, he travelled to Berlin and Moscow with Eddie Izzard to perform two shows to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the end of World War II.  
  
**Beyond comedy:**

He writes for outlets like THE ATLANTIC, THE BELIEVER, and THE NEW YORK TIMES, has written and produced documentaries for the US State Department and ESPN’s 30 FOR 30 series, and spent more than a decade working as a speechwriter for the Los Angeles Dodgers and the Boston Red Sox. Alex was named to the Time100 list of the world’s most influential people in 2024.

He likes ramen a whole lot.

## Solo Shows

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ec9852b9d9c412a9d842053/a8618884-043f-4315-8afd-7dbdac6f49cb/alexedelman_justforus_11x17_greenwichhousetheater_2022_RGB.jpeg)

## JUST FOR US

*A show about a meeting of Nazis that Edelman* — *an Orthodox Jew* — *attended,* JUST FOR US *was the best reviewed comedy show at the Edinburgh Fringe in more than a decade.*

Selected performances/runs:

* **2018:** Soho Theater (London), Subculture (New York), Melbourne Comedy Festival (Australia), Just for Laughs Festival (Montreal), Edinburgh Fringe Festival, Soho Theater return, The Bell House (New York).
* **2019:** The Virgil (Los Angeles), Aspen Laughs Festival (Aspen, CO), The Lyric Theater on La Brea (Los Angeles), Union Hall (New York).
* **2020:** Lucille Lortel Theater (New York), UK tour, truncated by COVID.
* **2021:** off-Broadway, Cherry Lane Theatre (!), Dec 1, 2021 - Feb 20, 2022.
* **2022:** Cherry Lane Theatre ends Feb 20. Soho Playhouse from March 23 - April 30. Greenwich Street Theatre June 13 - September 1. Williamstown Theatre Festival August 1 - 8. Woolly Mammoth (Washington, DC) November 16 - December 23.
* **2023:** Menier Chocolate Factory (London) - Jan 11 to Feb 26.

  Boston - April 11 - 23. Emerson Colonial, Boston, May 20. Broadway, the Hudson Theater - June 22 - August 19.

* **2024:** Released as a special on HBO’s Max on April 6.

Awards:

* Obie Award - **WINNER** - Creation and Performance
* Helen Hayes Award (DC) - Nominee
* Herald Angel Award
* Edinburgh Comedy Award - Nominee
* Barry Award for Best Show (Melbourne) - Nominee
* Outer Critics Circle Award - Nominee
* Drama Desk Award - Nominee
* Tony Award - **WINNER -** Special Tony
* Emmy Award - **WINNER** - Writing for a Scripted Variety Special

“Critics’ Pick! a belly-laugh funny show. A brisk, smart provocation of a monologue.”

*- The New York Times*

- Among Edelman’s many strengths as a writer and performer is an exceptional eye for the absurd.”  
 *- The New Yorker*

★★★★

Edelman is a master.

- *Time Out New York*

![IMG-0479.jpg](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ec9852b9d9c412a9d842053/1593197760659-11EUU2TBLC9XNVQEP5WK/IMG-0479.jpg)

## EVERYTHING HANDED TO YOU

*The follow-up to “Millennial,” this show was about family, Jewish identity and the scarcity of plug sockets in airports. Unanimously well-received, the show was the second best reviewed effort at the Edinburgh Fringe, and sold out its entire run.*

*The show was adapted into an hour-long special as part of the “Live at the BBC” series. It is now available on Netflix UK.*  
  
Selected performances/runs:

* **2015**: Leicester Comedy Festival (Nominee, Best Show), Edinburgh Fringe, Scotland
* **2016**: Soho Theater (London), Melbourne Comedy Festival (Australia), Union Hall (New York)

“Every story is a precision-engineered Rolls-Royce of a routine, perfectly timed and constructed.” —Steve Bennett, *Chortle*

All reviews for the show can be found [here](https://www.comedy.co.uk/fringe/2015/alex_edelman/).

![IMG-0481.JPG](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5ec9852b9d9c412a9d842053/1593198235396-10UPIGZQI8YRS4V58ACT/IMG-0481.JPG)

## MILLENNIAL

*A show about traumatic stock photos and Generation Y, “Millennial” won an Edinburgh Comedy Award, the first American offering to do so since Arj Barker’s 1997 show.*

*The show was adapted into a 30-minute special for BBC R4, produced by Arnab Chanda.*

*An updated version is still occasionally performed.*Selected performances/runs:

* **2014**: Edinburgh Fringe, Scotland - (Edinburgh Comedy Award for Best Newcomer)
* **2015**: Soho Theater (London), Lund Comedy Festival (Sweden), Melbourne Comedy Festival (Australia), SubCulture (New York), Meltdown (Los Angeles)

“Boasting technical flair and a precocious authority, Edelman's comedy manages to capture the voice of Generation Y.”

—*The Guardian*

### Galym Kaliakbarov in Europe. Stand-up tour 2026

##### Major European stand-up tour by Galym Kaliakbarov with a new concert program **Galym Kaliakbarov** is one of the most recognizable stand-up comedians in Kazakhstan, the founder of Stand Up Astana, and an experienced performer. In 2026, he embarks on a major European stand-up tour with a brand-new concert program. Throughout his career, Galym has recorded five solo stand-up specials and performed at hundreds of venues, gaining a wide audience thanks to his honest, precise, and intelligent humor. His short videos about family life, relationships, work, and everyday situations attract millions of views on social media and quickly go viral. Galym’s humor is built on sharp observations of real life in which audiences easily recognize themselves. He speaks about familiar topics directly, ironically, and accurately, creating a lively dialogue with the audience and provoking genuine laughter. His performances are distinguished by strong original material, confident delivery, and a high density of jokes. **The new stand-up concert as part of the 2026 European tour** offers an opportunity to spend an evening with an experienced comedian, hear fresh material, and experience high-level live stand-up. If you appreciate intelligent humor about family, relationships, and everyday life, this concert will be an excellent choice.

**Galym Kaliakbarov** is one of the most recognizable stand-up comedians in Kazakhstan, the founder of Stand Up Astana, and an experienced performer. In 2026, he embarks on a major European stand-up tour with a brand-new concert program.

Throughout his career, Galym has recorded five solo stand-up specials and performed at hundreds of venues, gaining a wide audience thanks to his honest, precise, and intelligent humor. His short videos about family life, relationships, work, and everyday situations attract millions of views on social media and quickly go viral.

Galym’s humor is built on sharp observations of real life in which audiences easily recognize themselves. He speaks about familiar topics directly, ironically, and accurately, creating a lively dialogue with the audience and provoking genuine laughter. His performances are distinguished by strong original material, confident delivery, and a high density of jokes.

**The new stand-up concert as part of the 2026 European tour** offers an opportunity to spend an evening with an experienced comedian, hear fresh material, and experience high-level live stand-up. If you appreciate intelligent humor about family, relationships, and everyday life, this concert will be an excellent choice.

![Image 8](https://uploads.europa24.ru/event/2026/spektakl-neznakomka-32-v-berline-69e9975a901c4.webp)

### Play "The Stranger 32" in Berlin: a comedy about the freedom that is missing

##### A live theatre performance about everyday life, choices, and an unexpected encounter that can change everything 

On the stage of the theater **«Antrepriza» in Berlin** the audience is awaiting **the play «Stranger 32»** — a contemporary comedy that explores the theme of a person's inner lack of freedom in ordinary everyday life.

At the center of the plot are characters who at first glance live ordinary lives, but in reality become hostages of circumstance. One is completely absorbed in work and loses touch with loved ones, another is under pressure from financial obligations and is forced to make risky decisions. Their existence is filled with routine, tension, and a sense of being at an impasse.

The situation changes when a mysterious Stranger appears in their lives — an embodiment of freedom, sincerity, and love for the world. Her arrival becomes a turning point, sparking an inner struggle and a rethinking of what is happening. **The main question of the play** — can love and humanity lead people out of the vicious circle and restore their sense of real life.

The production combines a light comedic format with deep content, raising themes of personal responsibility, values, and choice. It is a play that both entertains and provokes thought, inviting the viewer to look at their own life from a new angle.

The play is performed in Russian, age restriction — **16+**.

### Historical stand-up in Berlin (Germany) 2026

##### Historical stand-up in Germany (Berlin) — April 29, 2026 

On April 29 in Berlin there will be a **historical stand-up** — an unusual format that lets you look at the past with ease and humor.

It's a chance to see history from an unexpected angle: without boring lectures, but with vivid facts, irony, and lively interaction with the audience. The format combines informative content and stand-up, turning complex topics into an entertaining show.

Even if it doesn't change your life, you'll definitely be able to shine with interesting facts in the company of friends.

📍 The performance is in **German**

👶 Age restriction: **10+**

### “Dear Apartment” — Russian-language play in Berlin, 2026

##### Russian-language play in Berlin 2026. Tragicomedy, 18+ The play takes place in Saint Petersburg, in the Filatov family’s apartment. In the cozy dining room, as neighbor Sergey Ivanovich and Motya play chess, the cannon of the Peter and Paul Fortress suddenly fires. The play takes place in Saint Petersburg, in the Filatov family’s apartment. In the cozy dining room, as neighbor Sergey Ivanovich and Motya play chess, the cannon of the Peter and Paul Fortress suddenly fires.

The story follows an actress who played the lead role in a legendary fairy-tale film — a role that defined her destiny and forever changed her life.

**Creative team:**

Director — Alexander Mamontov

hits.snippet:
Paris for the first time since 1957. Tickets are 16 euros for adults, 8 euros reduced. [...] ## Food and Drink

Berlin Sommer Weinfest – June 14, 2026

Berlin’s most anticipated wine celebration is back this June, with a full day to explore over 350 wines from more than 70 winemakers from Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. Held at the Haus der Visionare, close to the Spree River and on the edge of Kreuzberg, the festival offers a small taste of a perfect summer atmosphere. Of course, this wine festival has the Berlin spin, with a DJ lineup curated by Anima Radio playing all afternoon and an afterparty in the evening. General admission is 30 euros.

Large pans of various dishes for serving at a Berlin food market

Large pans of various dishes for serving at a Berlin food market

Street Food auf Achse – Open Sundays from 12 to 6 PM [...] ## Outdoor fun

A colorful carnival ride at Berlin Vokfestsommar

Berliner Volkfestsommer – July 25 to August 17, 2026

Summer is hardly complete without a funfair, and Berlin has one right in the middle of summer. Over 60 rides both new and old offer thrills for all ages, with snacks and carnival games that have hardly changed since the first fair over 60 years ago. Located on fairgrounds in the northwest edge of Berlin, it’s a farther jaunt from the center, but definitely offers a full day’s worth of fun.

Kinderbauernhöfe at Görlitzer Park and Pankow – Open daily

![Visit home page of Insight Cities]()
![Visit home page of Insight Cities]()

# Summer 2026 Events in Berlin: A Ton of Festivals and Outdoor Fun

## **Top things to do in Berlin Summer 2026**

Nothing is quite epic as a Berlin summer. What the city lacks in older historic edifices, it makes up with an uber sense of modernity, manifested in urban festivals, experimental art and innovative live music. Insight Cities’ list of what’s hot this summer in Berlin puts you on the right track in this cosmopolitan European capital city.

![three people smiling in front of large memorial gate with statue on top](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

![three people smiling in front of large memorial gate with statue on top](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

## **Take a tour with Insight Cities**

First time in Berlin this summer? Take a tour with us at Insight Cities. Our [Berlin Introduction Tour](https://www.insightcities.com/tour/introduction-to-berlin-tour/) covers the main sights that testify to the city’s most significant chapters, with an expert guide sharing the dramatic stories behind them. All our local historians not only have bona fides in relevant fields but also convey Berlin’s fascinating eras in a way you can relate to. Book with us today and we’ll match you with the perfect guide for your group and interests.

## **Culture Festivals**

![a woman wearing a costume and holding props standing in front of colored lights at 48 hour Neukolln, Berlin](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

**[48 Stunden Neukölln](https://48-stunden-neukoelln.de/en) –July 3 to 5, 2026**

Berlin’s hippest district Neukölln (move over, Kreuzberg), celebrates its fascination for the arts each June with the rather quirky and imaginative “48 Hours Neukölln” art festival. As the moniker indicates, it is 48 action-packed hours of artistic endeavors. From street art to murals, conferences, markets and parties, this bottom up, citizen-led initiative, really shows how much culture means to the Berliner identity. This year’s theme covers borders between “outside” and “inside” — more specifically, the blurring between the two. Unmissable, with options for the whole family!

![A crowd of people browsing vendor stalls at the Kreuzberg Festival in Kreuzberg, Berlin, Germany](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

**[Kreuzberg Festival](https://kreuzberg-festival.de/) – June 26–28, 2026**

Every year, Kreuzberg celebrates the diversity of its neighborhood with a street festival. Local food vendors, artisans, and artists sell their wares, while performers of all types take the stage across three days. With the long summer days and convivial neighborhood atmosphere, the Kreuzberg festival is one of the best things to do in Berlin and fantastic for the whole family to check out.

Looking to explore more of the Kreuzberg neighborhood? Our [Kaleidoscopic Kreuzberg Tour](https://www.insightcities.com/tour/kaleidoscopic-kreuzberg-immigrants-artists-hipsters/) does just that. Starting at Oberbaumbruecke, Berlin’s most beautiful bridge, your urban historian guide will take you through the eastern sections of the neighborhood as well as the sweeping changes Kreuzberg has experienced, from its origins as a working class neighborhood in the 19th century, to an artistic haven attracting the likes of David Bowie and Iggy Pop, to the immigrant groups that have helped reshape the neighborhood in the past few decades. Along the way you’ll see remnants of the Berlin wall, notable street art, and Berlin’s renowned Markthalle 9. Get in touch with us at team@insightcities.com to get your guide today.

![A group of people marching and waving rainbow flag at Berlin's annual Pride Parade](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

**[Berlin Pride / Christopher Street Day Parade](https://csd-berlin.de/en) – July 25, 2026**

Berlin Pride, or the Christopher Street Day Parade, as it is colloquially known, gathers close to a million people on the streets in support of equal rights and self-determination. The show makes its way across the city from Kurfürstendamm to the proverbial city gates, Brandenburger Tor, as people walk, dance and drink, in support of the hundreds of floats that make up the parade. The street party begins at noon and officially ends at midnight, before it disperses into the streets around Brandenburger Tor. Bring your dancing shoes and remember to stay hydrated!

![A tall thin man standing next to a triangle, pointing at a picture he is holding](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

![A tall thin man standing next to a triangle, pointing at a picture he is holding](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

Berlin’s queer culture dates back well before the 20th century, and the city served as the forefront of advancements in gender theory in the days of the Weimar Republic. Our [Queer Berlin tour](https://www.insightcities.com/tour/queer-berlin-birthplace-of-lgbtq-movements/) uncovers much of this hidden history, with stops including the former site of the Institute of Sexual Science, founded by Magnus Hirschfield, the former El Dorado Cabaret, and the world’s first gay ghetto. Contact us to book your tour now.

**[Berlin Circus Festival](https://www.berlin-circus-festival.de/home.html) – August 5 – 16, 2026**

The Big Top sets up in the expansive Tempelhofer field for two weeks at the peak of summer. Berlin’s largest festival celebrating the circus arts include local and renowned international acts, with acrobats, jugglers and tightrope walkers performing gravity-defying stunts, pantomimes and clowns entertaining, and dancers showing the flexibility and expressiveness of the human body. Stay tuned for the full program!

**[Dance in August](https://www.tanzimaugust.de/en/) –  **August 13–29, 2026****

This international contemporary dance festival encourages experimental and innovative choreographies, with many premiere performances as a part of the program. A diversity of styles, as well as international performers, ensure that there’s something for every dance fan throughout the festival. In addition to live performances, the festival gets rounded out with discussions, films, and exhibitions. 

**[Atonal](https://berlin-atonal.com/) – August 27–30, 2026**

Atonal underlines why Berlin continues to be at the cutting edge of modern culture. A festival dedicated to the sonic and visual arts, it brings together a variety of progressive and electronic musicians and visual artists. What you get are unique, site-specific audio-visual displays, designed for the show and never repeated, created by some of the biggest names in the business. Atonal takes place at the massive, industrial Kraftwerk complex (a former power station, worth a visit by itself) and is well worth the 150 Euro ticket.

## **Music**

**[Open-air Concert with the Berlin Philharmonic](https://www.berliner-philharmoniker.de/en/concert/calendar/55679/) — June 27, 2026, 8:15 PM**

Every summer, the Berlin Philharmonic ends their performance season with a fantastic open-air concert at the expansive Waldbühne in Olympia Park. This year, the Waldbühne will transform into an Italian oasis, with tenor Jonas Kaufmann performing some of the most moving Italian arias along with the orchestra.

![Musicians performing before a crowd in the center of Berlin, Germany, as a part of the Fete de la musique event, on a bright city day, with the Reichstag in the background.](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

**[Fete de la Musique](https://www.fetedelamusique.de/en/) – June 21, 2026**

The European Festival of Music, on World Music Day, takes on a particular Berlin flavor. The streets are quite literally filled with the sounds of music, as Berliners and musicians of all genres, ages, shapes and sizes, take to the streets and have a ball. The festival is a refreshing change from all the free techno that abounds in the city, with its focus on live instrumental music. 

**[Klassik Open Air](https://www.britzergarten.de/events/eventkalender/) at Britzer Garden – August 22, 2026**

Britzer Garden closes out the summer with a bang, marking the evening with exuberant carnival-themed musical numbers. The New Frankfurt Philharmonic performs works by Dvořák, Rossini, and Verdi on stage before the lake. The evening concludes with a bombastic fireworks display lighting up the darkening sky.

![A band playing in front of an audience at Pop Kultur Fest in Berlin, with pink, purple and orange colored lights](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

**[Pop Kultur](https://www.pop-kultur.berlin/en/) – August 24 – 29, 2026**

Pop Kultur is an interdisciplinary festival of the arts and music, which gives visitors a chance to see some of the biggest and most creative names in the fields,  as well as a peek into what goes on behind the scenes. The festival is made up of three components: Live – with music, lectures, exhibitions and discussions, Talent – where the top talent in the fields come together, and Network – a conference for those in the creative industries; and it provides unique insight into how the cultural industry in Berlin operates. Taking place at the Kulturbrauerei in Prenzlauer Berg, among other venues, this year’s edition features musicians from Armenia, Azerbaijan and Georgia as well as many major German acts. Tickets start as low as 10 euro and will sell out well in advance.

## **Special Events**

**[Botanical Night](https://www.berlin.de/en/events/2962619-2842498-botanical-night-at-botanical-garden.en.html) – July 18, 2026**

There’s always something magical about visiting a daytime destination at night, and one night a year, the Berlin Botanical Gardens allows visitors to witness that nocturnal magic. Several themed areas illuminate an ethereal atmosphere evoking the enchantment of nature at night.

![View of Berlin's Bode Museum on Museuminsel, with the Berlin TV tower in the background](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

**[Long Night of the Museums](https://www.berlin.de/en/events/2091757-2842498-long-night-of-museums.en.html) – August 29, 2026**

The Long Night of the Museums gives you a chance to take in as many of Berlin’s museums as you can over the course of a single night. With hundreds of events on offer at 75 different museums, and a shuttle bus that lets you conveniently hop around the city till 2 A.M, plan ahead to make the best of your night. Other cities may have aped its style, but we’ll have you know that it was in Berlin where the idea first emanated. The event is put together by the museums and the city’s cultural department, Kulturprojekte Berlin.

## **Food and Drink**

**[Berlin Sommer Weinfest](https://www.berlinweinfest.de/) – June 14, 2026**

Berlin’s most anticipated wine celebration is back this June, with a full day to explore over 350 wines from more than 70 winemakers from Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. Held at the Haus der Visionare, close to the Spree River and on the edge of Kreuzberg, the festival offers a small taste of a perfect summer atmosphere. Of course, this wine festival has the Berlin spin, with a DJ lineup curated by Anima Radio playing all afternoon and an afterparty in the evening. General admission is 30 euros.

![Large pans of various dishes for serving at a Berlin food market](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

![Large pans of various dishes for serving at a Berlin food market](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

**[Street Food auf Achse](https://www.facebook.com/streetfoodaufachse/) – Open Sundays from 12 to 6 PM**

If you’re tired of the city’s overcrowded and rapidly-gentrifying market halls, look no further than Street Food auf Achse. Despite its tiny Prenzlauer Berg location inside the Kulturbrauerei, this is hands-down the city’s best street food show. With refreshingly different twists on dozens of international cuisines, presented by both amateur and professional chefs ranging from grandmothers to the occasional Michelin-star awardee, you can’t go wrong here.

**[African Food Festival](https://www.facebook.com/africanfoodfestivalberlin/) – July 18 & 19, 2026**

The African Food Festival is a rager. If you’re looking for your fill of injera, yam and shitto, tagines and countless other exotic African foods, this is where you should focus your attention. Join the city’s various African communities as they come out for a weekend in the sun, with food, alcohol, great music, dance and lots of love to share. It takes place at the intriguing Wilhelmian industrial complex, the Malzfabrik Schöneberg, this year.

## **Exhibitions**

![Women in traditional Serbian dresses dancing on grass in the dark while it rains in a performance by Marina Abramokic, to be shown in Berlin in summer 2026](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

[**Marina Abramović’s Balkan Erotic Epic**](https://www.berlinerfestspiele.de/en/gropius-bau/programm/2026/ausstellungen/marina-abramovic) **at Gropius Brau – closes August 23, 2026** 

Inspired by folklore from the Balkans, her region of origin, Marina Abramović presents a series of sculptures, installations and performances that explores eroticism that expands the sense of self, bridging life and death, self and cosmos. The result sometimes treads the line between sacred and profane, the horrific and the beatific. In any case, transformation and self-affirmation contain threads of meaning throughout her works.  This exhibition is sure to be one of the highlights of Berlin this summer. Tickets are 15 euros for adults, 10 euros reduced.

**[Art, People, Power](https://www.visitberlin.de/en/event/art-people-power-schonhausen-palace-scene-nazi-campaign-against-degenerate-art): Schönhausen Palace as the scene of the Nazi campaign against ‘degenerate art’ at Schönhausen Palace – opens June 12, 2026**

Close to the edge of Berlin stands Schönhausen Palace, an opulent Baroque summer palace largely unscathed by World War II. Among the palace’s many functions throughout history, it served as the storage facility for confiscated art work during the Third Reich. This new permanent exhibition explores the process of the Nazi Regime’s “degenerate art” campaign, from the people who confiscated the art to those who sold and bought it, to the affected artists themselves – all in the place where it happened.

[**Cassirer and the Breakthrough of Impressionism**](https://www.smb.museum/en/museums-institutions/alte-nationalgalerie/exhibitions/detail/cassirer-and-the-breakthrough-of-impressionism/) **at the Alte Nationalgalerie – until September 27, 2026**

Berlin-based gallerist Paul Cassirer was one of the most important art dealers of his time, playing an influential role in elevating French Impressionist painters such as Edgar Degas, Paul Cezanne, Edourad Manet, Claude Monet, August Renoir and Vincent van Gogh in Germany. More than 100 notable works by both French and German Impressionist painters, including those who were promoted by Cassirer, will be featured in this major exhibition, making this one a must for art lovers visiting Berlin this summer. Tickets are 14 euros for adults, 10 euros reduced.

![Sculpture "La Muse endormie" by artist Brancusi, a golden head lying on its side as if sleeping](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

[**Brancusi**](https://www.smb.museum/en/museums-institutions/kulturforum/exhibitions/detail/brancusi/) **at Neue Nationalgalerie – until August 9, 2026**

Romanian-born sculptor Constantin Brancusi (1876–1957) made his career in France and became one of the most influential sculptors of the 20th century. His distinct abstract sculptures, inspired by organic forms, influenced the development of sculptural abstraction, playing with different materials and surfaces, the effects of light and movement on a sculpture, and even staging. This exhibition features over 150 sculptures, photographs, drawings, films, and archival materials, many of them from the Centre Pompidou, which hosts much of his work. Notable works such as “The Kiss”, “Bird in Space”, “Sleeping Muse”, and “Endless Column” will be on view, as well as a partial reconstruction of Brancusi’s legendary studio, shown outside of Paris for the first time since 1957. Tickets are 16 euros for adults, 8 euros reduced.

## **Outdoor fun**

![A colorful carnival ride at Berlin Vokfestsommar](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

**[Berliner Volkfestsommer](https://volksfest-berlin.de/berliner-volksfest-sommer/) – July 25 to August 17, 2026**

Summer is hardly complete without a funfair, and Berlin has one right in the middle of summer. Over 60 rides both new and old offer thrills for all ages, with snacks and carnival games that have hardly changed since the first fair over 60 years ago. Located on fairgrounds in the northwest edge of Berlin, it’s a farther jaunt from the center, but definitely offers a full day’s worth of fun.

**[Kinderbauernhöfe](https://www.kinderbauernhofberlin.de/) at Görlitzer Park and Pankow – Open daily**

Kinderbauernhöfe are Berlin speak for the small urban farmyards that double as petting zoos and activity centers for kids in the summer. Our favorite is the one at Kreuzberg’s Görlitzer Park, which has ample playing space for kids and loads of fun activities to partake in. Make the visit a family fun day, with a roast chicken street food lunch, at the famous [Huhnerhaus 36](https://www.instagram.com/huhnerhaus36/), located at the edge of the park.

![A bike resting against a life raft rack on a lakeside beach in Berlin, Germany](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

![A bike resting against a life raft rack on a lakeside beach in Berlin, Germany](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAMLCwgAAACH5BAAAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

**Go for a swim in a natural lake**

Berlin may be a cosmopolitan capital but it also is full of green spaces and deep-blue lakes – and the swimming lakes are fairly sizeable in themselves. Lake Muggelsee is Berlin’s largest, with space enough for an aquatic playground in the shallow end as well as paddleboarding, sailing and even surfing. You also can’t go wrong with Wansee, with a white-sand beach and chic 1920s architecture.

**Picnic at a park with a view**

When the sun is high and the air is hot, locals and tourists alike flock to green spaces to wind down and chill out. Berlin is full of parks where you can get out of the heat and still enjoy incredible views of the city. One of our favorites is Victoria Park, which at its higher elevation offers a view of Berlin like no other, along with unique features such as a monument commemorating the victory over Napoleon at the summit, as well as a cascading waterfall.

[Monbijoupark](https://www.visitberlin.de/en/monbijoupark) and James Simon Park, situated close to the Spree, offer plenty of space and a view of various famous Berlin sights, such as the impressive Berlin Cathedral with its famous domed roof and the TV Tower, which is a landmark of the Berlin skyline.

A picnic in the [Tiergarten](https://www.visitberlin.de/en/tiergarten) is also an ideal starting point for sightseeing, as the National Monument of the Wars of Unification is in the center of the park: the famous Victory Column with its bronze statue of Victoria. The community park Gleisdreieck park can be great for a picnic in Berlin, from which you can embark on sightseing, as the park sits close to many famous sights such as the Jewish Museum, Checkpoint Charlie, and Potsdamer Platz.

If you want to get more into the weeds of Berlin’s history and culture, [contact us](https://www.insightcities.com/contact-us/) at Insight Cities to arrange your tour. We have guides who know Berlin’s rich multicultural heritage and turbulent history inside and out — and are fantastic storytellers on top of it all. Summer is the high touring season in Berlin, so don’t delay and get in touch with us to get your Berlin guide today!

## Описание мероприятия

Хочешь провести вечер с живым юмором, где можно просто расслабиться и посмеяться от души?

Добро пожаловать на Открытый Микрофон от комиктива Стендап Берлин — самое тёплое и непредсказуемое русскоязычное стендап-шоу в Берлине!

Каждая среда — совершенно новое шоу! Комики самого разного уровня проверяют свои шутки, дошлифовывают старые, а также общаются с залом.

Комики говорят о всём, что волнует: работе, любви, технологиях, путешествиях, странных привычках и человеческих глупостях. Да о чём угодно, правда! У нас нет рамок — главное, чтобы было смешно.

Что вас ждёт:

 10+ комиков за вечер
 Живой, настоящий и спонтанный юмор
 Крутая атмосфера, где всем рады
 Тонны улыбок и хорошего настроения!

# Открытый микрофон. Стендап в Берлине с 22 апреля 2026 по 6 мая 2026

Хочешь провести вечер с живым юмором, где можно просто расслабиться и посмеяться от души? Добро пожаловать на Открытый Микрофон от комиктива Стендап Берлин — самое тёплое и непредсказуемое русскоязычное стендап-шоу в Берлине!

[Добавить в избранное](javascript:void(0) "Добавить в свои закладки на сайте")

Стендап проходит на русском языке

## Описание мероприятия

Хочешь провести вечер с живым юмором, где можно просто расслабиться и посмеяться от души?

Добро пожаловать на **Открытый Микрофон** от комиктива **Стендап Берлин** — самое тёплое и непредсказуемое русскоязычное стендап-шоу в Берлине!

Каждая среда — совершенно новое шоу! Комики самого разного уровня проверяют свои шутки, дошлифовывают старые, а также общаются с залом.

Комики говорят о всём, что волнует: работе, любви, технологиях, путешествиях, странных привычках и человеческих глупостях. Да о чём угодно, правда! У нас нет рамок — главное, чтобы было смешно.

Что вас ждёт:

* 10+ комиков за вечер
* Живой, настоящий и спонтанный юмор
* Крутая атмосфера, где всем рады
* Тонны улыбок и хорошего настроения!

 [Подробнее Свернуть](#tourSectionDesc)

Одни из них необходимы, другие наряду с пикселями и сопоставимыми технологиями помогают улучшить наш сайт и ваш опыт, а также способствуют отображению персонализированной рекламы. В рекламных целях эти данные могут передаваться третьим лицам, таким как поисковые системы, социальные сети или рекламные агентства.
